items.title: BIS Affiliate Rules (90 FR 47201) – Critical Suspension Countdown (Nov 10, 2026)
items.summary: BIS Affiliate Rules remains in one-year suspension window. Any foreign entity owned 50%+ in aggregate by Entity List/MEU List parties will be automatically captured. Weekly focus: beneficial ownership tracing and screening. Re-imposition date: November 10, 2026.

impact_assessment.scenario: Foreign affiliate entity ownership crossing 50% threshold triggers automatic licensing requirements

items.title: Digital & Cloud Controls: Remote Access to US Compute Clusters (AI/ML Servers)
items.summary: Following RASA and January 2026 Final Rule: providing remote access to high-end US compute clusters is classified as export of hardware capability. IaaS providers are liable for 'knowing' remote user identity. Compute aggregation monitoring required across entire clusters, not individual chips.

impact_assessment.scenario: Cloud provider liability for unauthorized remote access to controlled computing resources

items.title: ECCN & Technical Thresholds: Encryption, Open Source, De Minimis Analysis
items.summary: Hardware with strong encryption (5A002) requires License Exception ENC analysis. Software in private repositories (Llama 2/3) is subject to EAR. De minimis rule: foreign items with >10% controlled US content remain under US jurisdiction for most destinations.

impact_assessment.scenario: Unauthorized export or provision of controlled encryption or open-source software

items.title: US AI Executive Order Implementation: 90-Day Review & Emerging Standards
items.summary: Just Security analysis of the US Administration's AI executive order progress, focusing on federal AI governance, agency coordination, and emerging international regulatory alignment. Covers implementation timelines and compliance expectations for organizations interacting with US federal systems.

items.title: AI Geopolitics: US-China Competition & Third Country Regulatory Dynamics
items.summary: Analysis of how US-China AI competition is shaping global regulatory responses. Examines export controls, talent competition, and how regional jurisdictions (EU, UK, UAE, Singapore) are positioning themselves in the AI governance landscape.

items.title: NIST AI RMF Implementation: Security & Risk Management in Practice
items.summary: Just Security's guide to implementing NIST AI Risk Management Framework within organizations. Includes case studies of regulatory compliance, third-party integration, and practical security assessment methodologies for AI systems.

items.title: Corporate Liability for AI Systems: Board-Level Governance & Risk Oversight
items.summary: Analysis of emerging corporate liability frameworks for AI systems, including board oversight responsibilities, disclosure requirements, and insurance implications. Covers recent SEC guidance and emerging enforcement actions.

items.title: Data Rights in AI Training: Privacy Regulator Views & Emerging Frameworks
items.summary: Comprehensive overview of how privacy regulators (EU, UK, California) are addressing data rights in AI training and large language models. Includes analysis of consent frameworks, training data licensing, and cross-border transfer constraints.

items.title: OFAC enforcement settlement with TradeStation Securities
items.summary: OFAC announced a settlement related to sanctions compliance failures. Firms should review screening controls, escalation procedures, and exposure to US persons and USD clearing.

impact_assessment.scenario: Enforcement action triggered by screening gaps or USD exposure

items.title: ADGM warning on false claims of regulatory status
items.summary: ADGM issued a public alert regarding entities falsely claiming ADGM authorisation. Firms should strengthen counterparty verification procedures.

impact_assessment.scenario: Counterparty failure or reputational damage from association with false-licensed entity

items.title: UAE guidance on responsible AI governance expectations
items.summary: UAE authorities issued guidance emphasising transparency, human oversight, and accountability in AI deployment. Organisations should map AI use cases, implement governance controls, and document risk assessments.

impact_assessment.scenario: Operational AI system deemed non-compliant with governance standards

items.title: Expanded export control scrutiny for cross‑border technology transfers
items.summary: Regulators are increasing scrutiny of cross‑border technology transfers involving advanced computing, data access, and cloud infrastructure. Companies should reassess vendor access, data residency, and licensing requirements.

impact_assessment.scenario: Undisclosed technology transfer or failure to obtain export license

items.title: UK regulator signals stricter approach to data governance
items.summary: Pinsent Masons reports on UK regulatory signals indicating increased enforcement focus on data governance and accountability. Organisations should review data handling policies and escalation procedures.

items.title: DIFC Courts judgment on regulatory compliance breach
items.summary: The DIFC Courts issued a judgment addressing compliance failures in a regulated entity. The case highlights expectations around governance, internal controls, and regulatory disclosures within the DIFC framework.
